agent_reply: Implemented the blocking startup error screen.

agent_reply: - Moved the ErrorView icon down from `y=4` to `y=24`.

commit_message: good progress working on GUI but ran into memory problems with PNG decode

user_prompt: avoid having `M5GFX& display` as a parameter for functions and methods, there should be a global reference to the display for anything unrelated to FlyGui, and if FlyGui is involved, there should be a way to dig through trees to get a reference to it, or just use the global reference
agent_reply: - Updated direct display users to use `thefly_display` instead of threading a local `display` reference or calling `M5.Display` directly.
- Kept `FlyGui::display()` as a convenience getter that returns the global display, so tree-based GUI code can still ask the GUI for its screen if needed.

user_prompt: This will involve `lib\SpriteDraw\SpriteDraw.cpp` `SpriteDraw::drawPng` and `draw_png_run` and `argb_to_rgb565` and maybe others.

I want to add a parameter for darkening or dithering a sprite. I want to be able to draw icons but either darkened or dithered with black, as to indicate "this item is inactive", without having a pile of extra graphic assets.

`SpriteDraw::drawPng` takes this as a parameter, `PngDecodeContext` carries this setting around to `draw_png_run`. `argb_to_rgb565` takes in a new parameter. If dither flag is involved, then right before `thefly_display.drawPixel(pixel_x, dst_y, argb_to_rgb565(argb));`, we think if Y is even, then X must be even, if Y is odd, X must be odd, otherwise draw black.
and fix up any mistakes in that file
agent_reply: - Dither mode draws normal/darkened pixels only when `(x & 1) == (y & 1)`, otherwise black.
- Works in both single-pixel and run/chunk drawing paths.
SpriteDraw::drawPng(data, bytes, x, y, w, h, true,

I attempted a PlatformIO build, but it failed in partition generation with `The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect`, not at the changed C++ files. No orphan build processes remain.

user_prompt: find me places where we are not using 1.0f
agent_reply: Places currently using non-`1.0f` text scaling:
`kTextSize = 2.0f`, used for duration and caller info.

Worth noting: [FlyGuiText.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGuiText.cpp:201) calls `setTextSize(fontSize_)`, so any `FlyGuiText` instance can be scaled indirectly. The top bar date/time uses `1.0f`; the RecordingView text instances are the non-`1.0f` ones I found.

For all informative screen main text, like the waiting screens, error screens, ModalDialog, use font 2 size 1. The text at the bottom is perfect as is

commit_message: more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: originally I designed buttons to be 106x114 sized because it fit a 3x2 layout
then, the RecordingView was designed and I decided on the thick border and for that some 100x100 sized graphics made more sense
and the modal dialogs and error dialogs use... wild sizes
it's kind of a pain in the ass to manage these different sizes

so I added a tool to automatically resize them from original high resolution art with rules about margins and centering and naming, and want to normalize most of the graphics to be 100x100 with only some exceptions
I have regenerated `inc\sprites.h` and `src\sprites.cpp` while I kept `inc\sprites.h.old` as a reference for the replacements
So the logic in `MainScreenView` needs to account for these changes and keep the graphics in the same locations
Various dialog-like views need their top-center graphics changed to use the 100x100 versions, the text position should follow automatically
There are probably other changes that are required
agent_reply: Changed the UI code to use the regenerated 100x100 symbols across:
- Main screen 100x100 graphics are inset inside the old 106x114 grid so their visual centers stay put.
- Modal/error dialog top graphics now use 100x100 assets, with error text positioned from the sprite height instead of a stale hard-coded old graphic size.
- Connection waiting graphics switched from old 180x150 art to 100x100 centered at the old visual center.
- Removed stale references to deleted `btn_*`, `*_180x150`, `error_large`, `exit_50`, and `handshake_60` symbols.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly` succeeds. The only warnings were existing/library warnings, not from these sprite changes.

user_prompt: ok I put the missing sprites in, let's do this again
for the `ErrorView` I want a change
I want to place a button with `sprite_doorreturn_50` for non fatal errors, clicking it or `BtnC` is now what dismisses the dialog. Remove the bottom message.

For fatal errors, place a button with `sprite_firstaidwifi_50` and when clicking it, it does a best effort at launching the default secure soft AP and then showing the `WifiApModeView` . The polling loop for this will need to have `WifiManager` polling, and maybe `BattTracker::poll();` too.
Only show this new Wi-Fi button if `wifi_manager` is not null.

Make sure that the launching of the default secure softAP does not require the successful loading of NVS. I understand that a lot of the web functionality might fail, like `/get_info` or `/get_cfg`, make sure those fail gracefully with HTTP codes when NVS is not loaded. We do need the file uploader to work though.
agent_reply: - Nonfatal errors now show `sprite_doorreturn_50`; only that button or `BtnC` dismisses.
- Removed the bottom “touch screen” / fatal restart text.
- Fatal errors now show `sprite_firstaidwifi_50` when `wifi_manager` exists.
- The fatal Wi-Fi button best-effort starts the generated secure SoftAP, starts the web server, and switches to `WifiApModeView`.
- Fatal error polling now keeps `gui`, `BattTracker`, and `WifiManager` moving.
- NVS init failure now stops later, after `wifi_manager` and microSD can exist, so the rescue AP/file upload path can come up.
- `/get_info`, `/get_cfg`, `/set_cfg`, password reset, and memory reset now return `503` when NVS/config state is unavailable. File upload/list/download handlers remain independent.
One note: plain `pio run` still fails because the default `thefly` env does not define `BUILD_WITH_SECURITY_LEVEL`; the security-specific envs are good.

commit_message: better error screens with rescue wifi

user_prompt: also make sure every `.cpp` file inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` meet the guidelines at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`, however, it is also important that their code layout look almost the same between them as they do kind of inherit from the same base classes sometimes
agent_reply: The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.
- `src\version.c` build churn was restored; final status only shows the intended layout files.
